skill.name: patent-claim-mapper
skill.description: Use when mapping patent claims to products, analyzing patent infringement, or preparing freedom-to-operate analyses. Compares patent claims against product features for biotech and pharmaceutical IP assessment.
skill.summary: Map patent claims to product features for infringement risk analysis and freedom-to-operate assessments in biotech and pharmaceutical contexts.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 79 static analysis findings evaluated and determined to be false positives. The skill performs text-based patent claim analysis using Jaccard similarity on keyword sets with no cryptographic operations, network calls, credential access, or shell code execution. Pattern matches in markdown documentation code blocks and variable names (e.g. 'key' in 'keywords') triggered the static analyzer but do not represent actual security risks. The script reads user-provided patent and product files and writes an analysis report as expected for a CLI tool.

content.user_title: Map patent claims to products for infringement analysis
content.value_statement: Patent analysts spend hours manually comparing claim language to product specifications. This skill automates claim-to-product mapping using keyword similarity analysis, producing structured infringement risk reports with clear coverage status for each claim element.
actual_capabilities: Parse patent claim text into structured elements with type classification (apparatus, method, composition, feature)
Extract keyword features from product descriptions and technical specifications
Compare patent claim elements against product features using Jaccard similarity scoring
Generate infringement risk reports with mapped, partial, and not-mapped status for each claim element
Calculate overall risk score and provide actionable recommendations based on coverage analysis
limitations: Does not interpret legal claim construction or perform Markman-style analysis
Similarity scoring is based on keyword overlap and does not understand semantic meaning
Cannot read patent drawings or diagrams, only text-based claims
Does not search patent databases or prior art; works only with provided claim text

use_cases.title: Freedom-to-Operate Screening
use_cases.description: A biotech patent analyst maps a competitor patent against their drug candidate to identify which claim elements may pose infringement risk before product launch.

use_cases.title: Competitive Patent Landscape
use_cases.description: An IP strategist compares multiple patents against a reference product to identify which patents have the highest coverage overlap for competitive intelligence reports.

use_cases.title: Due Diligence Support
use_cases.description: A legal researcher maps patent claims from an M&A target portfolio against the acquiring company product line to surface potential infringement exposure.

prompt_templates.title: Quick claim-to-product check
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a patent claim and a product description I need to compare. Let me use the patent-claim-mapper skill to parse the claim, extract the product features, and generate a mapping report showing which claim elements are covered.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user running a single infringement check

prompt_templates.title: FTO analysis with report output
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a freedom-to-operate analysis for our new drug candidate. Please run the patent claim mapper using these claim texts and our product specification. Generate a structured report with risk levels and recommendations for each claim element.
prompt_templates.scenario: Patent analyst preparing an FTO memo

prompt_templates.title: Multi-patent portfolio comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a set of 5 competitor patents and one target product specification. For each patent, run the claim mapper and produce a summary table comparing which claims are mapped, partially mapped, or not mapped to our product features.

prompt_templates.title: Custom threshold and gap analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the patent claim mapper with a custom similarity threshold of 0.6 for partial mapping. For elements that are not mapped at all, identify the specific claim language that has no corresponding product feature and flag these for design-around investigation.

output_examples.input: Independent claim 1: A monoclonal antibody that specifically binds to human PD-1 and blocks the interaction with PD-L1.
output: Claim Element: A monoclonal antibody that specifically binds to human PD-1
Product Feature: Anti-PD-1 therapeutic antibody with PD-L1 blockade
Risk: High - direct match on mechanism of action

output_examples.input: Dependent claim 5: The antibody of claim 1, wherein the antibody is a human IgG1 isotype.
output: Claim Element: Antibody is a human IgG1 isotype
Risk: Low - different antibody isotype may avoid literal infringement

best_practices: Provide full patent claim text including preamble, transitional phrases, and dependent claims for complete coverage analysis
Verify product descriptions include all technical specifications, mechanism of action details, and formulation information for accurate feature extraction
Review partial mappings manually since keyword overlap scoring may miss semantically equivalent but lexically different terms
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for claim construction or validity opinions - it only measures text similarity, not legal interpretation
Do not rely solely on the risk score without human review - the keyword-based algorithm can produce false matches from coincidental word overlap
Do not assume the skill reads PDF images or drawings - it only processes text content from patent documents

faq.question: What types of patents can this skill analyze?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes any text-based patent claims. It works best with utility patents in biotech, pharma, and medical device domains where claim language maps clearly to product features.

faq.question: Does this skill provide a legal opinion on infringement?
faq.answer: No. The skill performs text similarity analysis and generates a structured report. Infringement is a legal determination that requires human legal expertise and claim construction analysis.

faq.question: What file formats are supported for input?
faq.answer: The skill accepts plain text input. You can provide claim text directly or from TXT files. PDF text extraction must be done separately before using this skill.

faq.question: How is the similarity score calculated?
faq.answer: The score uses Jaccard similarity, which measures keyword overlap between claim elements and product features. A score above 0.7 indicates mapped, 0.4 to 0.7 is partial, and below 0.4 is not mapped.

faq.question: Can this skill handle multiple patents at once?
faq.answer: The core analysis works on one patent claim set at a time. For multi-patent portfolios, run the analysis separately for each patent and combine results manually or with a summary prompt.

faq.question: Does the skill store or transmit my patent data?
faq.answer: No. The skill performs all analysis locally using text processing. No data is sent to external servers or stored beyond the analysis report you choose to save.
